title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer flagged 4 warnings: (1) Add a `helm fail` guard to prevent the invalid combination of `rbac.namespaceScoped=true` with `webhook.enabled=true`, which currently produces a broken deployment with confusing runtime 403 errors instead of a clear install-time failure. (2) Replace `image.tag=latest` in the multi-tenant deployment docs with a pinned version tag. The other two warnings were cut off but the key theme is enforcing constraints at template render time rather than letting users discover misconfigurations at runtime.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 8cf1d5c with Claude CLI

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=0 critical=0 warning=4 info=2 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## WARNING (4)

### W-001: No template-level guard for incompatible rbac.namespaceScoped=true + webhook.enabled=true
**File**: `templates/role.yaml:1` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Helm Values Validation

**Problem**: The documentation states users must set webhook.enabled: false when rbac.namespaceScoped: true, but the Helm templates do not enforce this. A user who sets rbac.namespaceScoped=true without disabling webhooks will get a Role (no ClusterRole) alongside cluster-scoped MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration resources. The operator pod will start but fail at runtime trying to manage cluster-scoped webhook configs it has no RBAC for, producing a confusing 403 error deep in the reconcile loop.

**Code**:
```
{{- if .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}
```

**Action Required**: Add a fail guard in a _helpers.tpl or at the top of deployment.yaml so that helm template and helm install fail immediately with a clear message instead of producing a broken deployment.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
{{- if and .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped .Values.webhook.enabled }}
{{- fail "rbac.namespaceScoped=true requires webhook.enabled=false — namespace-scoped operators cannot manage cluster-scoped webhook configurations" }}
{{- end }}
{{- if .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}
```

---

### W-002: Documentation example uses image.tag=latest
**File**: `docs/reference/multi-tenant-deployment.md:100` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Helm Image Patterns, Kubernetes Image Management

**Problem**: The install example uses --set image.tag=latest which contradicts Helm and Kubernetes best practices. latest is a mutable tag that destroys deployment reproducibility and makes rollback ambiguous.

**Code**:
```
  --set image.repository=ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone-operator \
  --set image.tag=latest \
  --wait --timeout 120s
```

**Action Required**: Replace image.tag=latest with a placeholder SemVer tag like v0.1.0.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
  --set image.repository=ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone-operator \
  --set image.tag=v0.1.0 \
  --wait --timeout 120s
```

---

### W-003: CRD install command will not work — CRDs lack the app.kubernetes.io/component=crd label
**File**: `docs/reference/multi-tenant-deployment.md:162-168` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: likely — **Pattern**: Cross-reference documentation examples against implementation, Verify documented commands work end-to-end

**Problem**: The documented CRD-only installation command uses --selector 'app.kubernetes.io/component=crd', but CRD YAML files in crds/ are raw Kubernetes manifests without Helm label injection. CRDs rendered by helm template --include-crds do not receive chart labels — they are passed through verbatim. The --selector will match zero resources, and no CRDs will be installed. A user following this command gets a silent no-op.

**Code**:
```
helm template keystone-operator \
  operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/ \
  --include-crds | kubectl apply --server-side -f - \
  --selector 'app.kubernetes.io/component=crd'
```

**Action Required**: Remove the selector and pipe only CRDs, or use a simpler approach such as kubectl apply --server-side -f operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Replace the command with a direct CRD apply:

kubectl apply --server-side -f \
  operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/
```

---

### W-004: Missing E2E Tests for namespace-scoped RBAC
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/:1` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Ensure test names accurately describe their coverage scope, Missing E2E Tests

**Problem**: The project has Chainsaw E2E tests for every existing feature (15 directories under tests/e2e/keystone/). This PR adds a significant new deployment mode — namespace-scoped RBAC — but includes no E2E test for it.

**Code**:
```
tests/e2e/keystone/
```

**Action Required**: Add a Chainsaw test that deploys the operator with rbac.namespaceScoped=true and webhook.enabled=false, creates a Keystone CR, and verifies reconciliation succeeds to validate the end-to-end flow.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add a new Chainsaw E2E test directory (e.g. tests/e2e/keystone/namespace-scoped-rbac/) that deploys the operator with rbac.namespaceScoped=true and webhook.enabled=false, creates a Keystone CR, and asserts successful reconciliation.
```

---

## INFO (2)

### I-001: Role and ClusterRole duplication lacks reciprocal cross-reference
**File**: `templates/role.yaml:2-3` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Flag duplicated logic blocks with sync cross-references

**Problem**: The role.yaml comment 'Rules MUST be kept in sync with clusterrole.yaml' is good, but clusterrole.yaml has no reciprocal cross-reference. When a future PR adds a new RBAC rule to clusterrole.yaml, the author may not realize role.yaml also needs updating.

**Code**:
```
# CC-0043: Namespace-scoped Role (mirrors ClusterRole rules).
# Rules MUST be kept in sync with clusterrole.yaml.
```

**Action Required**: Add a reciprocal comment at the top of clusterrole.yaml referencing role.yaml.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
{{- if not .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}
# NOTE: Rules MUST be kept in sync with role.yaml (CC-0043).
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
```

---

### I-002: namespace flag not logged at startup
**File**: `manager.go:137` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Kubernetes Operator Observability

**Problem**: When --namespace is set, the operator silently restricts its scope without any log message. At 3am when oncall is debugging why the operator only reconciles resources in one namespace, there is no log line to confirm namespace-scoped mode is active.

**Code**:
```
setupLog.Info("starting manager")
```

**Action Required**: Add a log line when namespace restriction is active, before the existing 'starting manager' log.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
	if namespace != "" {
		setupLog.Info("namespace-scoped mode enabled", "namespace", namespace)
	}
	setupLog.Info("starting manager")
```

---

## Summary

This is a well-structured PR with thorough Helm unit tests, comprehensive documentation, and clean Go code changes. The Role rules correctly mirror the ClusterRole rules. The most important issue is the missing fail guard for the incompatible rbac.namespaceScoped=true + webhook.enabled=true combination (W-001), which will silently produce a broken operator. The documented CRD install command (W-003) will also silently install nothing due to a non-existent label selector. Missing E2E test coverage for the new deployment mode is a notable gap given the project's otherwise thorough test suite.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| WARNING  | 4 |
| INFO     | 2 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Merge after fixing W-001 (template fail guard) and W-003 (broken CRD install command). W-002 (latest tag) and W-004 (missing E2E test) should also be addressed but could be follow-ups. INFO findings are optional improvements.
resolution_summary: All 6 review findings (4 warnings, 2 info) were addressed. W-001: a Helm fail guard was added to role.yaml to reject the incompatible rbac.namespaceScoped=true + webhook.enabled=true combination at template render time. W-002: image.tag=latest was replaced with image.tag=v0.1.0 in the docs example. W-003: the broken CRD install command using --selector was replaced with a direct kubectl apply on the crds/ directory. W-004: a Chainsaw E2E test was added at tests/e2e/keystone/namespace-scoped-rbac/. I-001: rather than just adding a reciprocal comment, RBAC rules were extracted into a shared named template 'keystone-operator.rbacRules' in _helpers.tpl, and both clusterrole.yaml and role.yaml now include it — eliminating duplication entirely (a reciprocal comment was also added to clusterrole.yaml). I-002: a startup log line 'namespace-scoped mode enabled' was added to manager.go before the existing 'starting manager' log. Additional changes not directly mentioned in the review: RBAC rules extraction into templates/_helpers.tpl was applied to both clusterrole.yaml and role.yaml to address I-001's duplication concern more thoroughly than the suggested reciprocal-comment fix.
